vocab.help_nuance: Dexters are a small, dual-purpose heritage breed from Ireland — real registered cattle, NOT the same thing as unregistered 'miniature' novelty cattle. They come in three colours (black, red, dun) and two body types: the short-legged (chondrodysplasia-carrying) type and the non-short ('long-legged'/proportionate) type. Two chondro carriers bred together risk the lethal 'bulldog' (PHA) calf, so responsible sellers disclose chondro and PHA status. Many Dexters are prized as A2/A2 milk and small-acreage cattle.

copy.roundupTitle: Every Dexter genetics listing, one feed
copy.roundupIntro: The Roundup brings the whole Dexter genetics market together: listings posted on DexterTank plus semen and embryo listings we track from breeders' public pages across the web (marked 📡 — those link straight back to the original seller's page; web sellers pay nothing and can remove a listing anytime).
copy.theaterIntro: The world's Dexter video library — bulls on film, sale recordings, and homestead how-to. Searchable by registration number.
copy.foundationIntro: Every Dexter traces back to the small hardy cattle of southwest Ireland — and to the breeders who carried them to Britain, America, and beyond. Meet the animals behind the breed.
